description:
You are the mobile build engineer for a continuous-integration (CI) environment whose working directory lives under /home/user.  
    A routine clean-up/refactor is required that can be performed with nothing more than the standard GNU find(1) + xargs(1) tool-chain (no third-party utilities).

    Current directory layout

      /home/user/mobile-build/
      ├── artifacts/          ← contains assorted *.apk / *.ipa build outputs
      └── logs/               ← contains plain-text *.log build logs

    The goals

    1. Create a text file called
         /home/user/reports/large_artifacts.txt
       that lists every *.apk or *.ipa file whose **actual size on disk is strictly larger than 20 MiB (20 * 1024 * 1024 = 20  971  520 bytes)**.

       • One absolute path per line (no extra spaces, no trailing slash).  
       • Sort the lines in normal ASCII lexical order (the same order produced by sort(1) with no flags).  
       • Do not include files that are 20 MiB or smaller.

    2. Gzip-compress every “old” build log.  
       A log is considered old when its modification-time is **more than 7 days in the past** at the moment your commands run.

       • Operate only on files in /home/user/mobile-build/logs/ that have the “.log” extension.  
       • Keep the original log files intact (i.e. use gzip -k or equivalent).  
       • The newly created compressed files must live in the same directory and be named identically except for the additional “.gz” suffix (e.g. build-20230901.log.gz).

    3. Write a machine-readable compression report to

         /home/user/reports/compression_summary.log

       The report must follow this exact format so that automated tests can parse it:

         <basename_of_compressed_file> <size_in_bytes_after_gzip>

       • One entry per line, covering **only** the logs that were actually compressed in step 2.  
       • Use the basename of the compressed file (e.g. “build-20230901.log.gz”, not a full path).  
       • Fields are separated by a single ASCII space.  
       • Lines can appear in any order.

    Notes & constraints

    • Accomplish everything using at most 3-4 simple shell commands that combine find, xargs (or find -exec), and coreutils.  
    • Do not hard-code any filenames—derive everything dynamically via find.  
    • The /home/user/reports/ directory may or may not exist yet; create it if necessary.  
    • The CI user does not have root privileges.  
    • The automated grader will verify:

        – large_artifacts.txt exists, is non-empty, and lists exactly the >20 MiB *.apk/*.ipa files that are present.  
        – Each listed path is real and sorted correctly.  
        – Every old *.log file (>7 days) now has a corresponding *.log.gz sibling.  
        – compression_summary.log exists, is non-empty, and each line follows the specified “basename size” pattern where size correctly reflects the current size of the .gz file at test time.

    Produce no additional files other than the two described report files and the .gz artifacts.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth set-up for the grader.
        The agent must *not* see anything inside this block.

        Directory tree and initial files that exist before the task starts
        (home path is /home/user):

        /home/user/mobile-build/artifacts/app-debug-1.apk        26214400 bytes
        /home/user/mobile-build/artifacts/app-release.apk        31457280 bytes
        /home/user/mobile-build/artifacts/old-test.ipa           29360128 bytes
        /home/user/mobile-build/artifacts/beta-small.apk         19922944 bytes   <-- must *not* be listed

        /home/user/mobile-build/logs/build-20230901.log          12000 bytes ; mtime = 10 days ago
        /home/user/mobile-build/logs/test-20230902.log           8000  bytes ; mtime = 9  days ago
        /home/user/mobile-build/logs/build-latest.log            15000 bytes ; mtime = now

        The grader will check:

        1. /home/user/reports/large_artifacts.txt

              Expected complete, lexicographically-sorted content:
                /home/user/mobile-build/artifacts/app-debug-1.apk
                /home/user/mobile-build/artifacts/app-release.apk
                /home/user/mobile-build/artifacts/old-test.ipa

           (Exactly three lines, Unix LF line endings.)

        2. Compressed logs:

              /home/user/mobile-build/logs/build-20230901.log.gz
              /home/user/mobile-build/logs/test-20230902.log.gz

           Both files must exist and be valid gzip files; original .log files must remain.

        3. /home/user/reports/compression_summary.log

           Must contain exactly two lines (order unimportant), each matching:

                build-20230901.log.gz <digits+>
                test-20230902.log.gz <digits+>

           Size field must match the actual stat --format=%s byte count of the .gz file that the agent just generated.

        The grader will *not* require any entry for build-latest.log because its mtime is <7 days.
    -->
